title: primulaceae
intro: The Primulaceae rank, also known as the Primrose family, is a taxonomic grouping of flowering plants that consists of approximately 1,200 species spread across 20 to 30 genera. This family is characterized by its diverse range of species, which exhibit various growth habits and flower forms, from low-growing, delicate primroses to tall, shrub-like plants. The Primulaceae family is primarily composed of herbaceous perennials and shrubs, with the majority of species being native to the Northern Hemisphere. Primulaceae species are often found in woodland environments and are known for their showy, often fragrant flowers that bloom in a variety of colors, including white, pink, and purple.
